interactions.text: Grandpa always said: rooms remember. This key's been hung facing the wall. Someone put it back in a hurry — or in the dark. And the dust says: recently.
The Ceremonial Key, back on its hook — facing the right way now, because Gary turned it around. Rooms remember. So do reporters.

detail.text: Edmund Vale at the sealing ceremony, fifty years young, one key raised high for the crowd. The painter caught everything: the smile, the morning light — and, at his belt, on a ribbon, something small that catches the varnish like brass.
detail.question: q_why_two_keys

detail.text: "THE SEALING OF THE CAPSULE — FOUNDING DAY." Edmund Vale raises the Ceremonial Key. The whole district walks under this painting weekly and mostly remembers to dust it.
The council chair. One armrest carries eight years of the same coat's wear; the other is nearly new. Warren sits leaning toward the door, like a man keeping a seat rather than holding one.
